observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Exert more efforts in retrieving the records of CIPs with missing details
and update the Construction in Progress Ledger Cards to accurately
support the Financial Statement balances;

b. Upon securing complete references/documents, prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the classification of completed projects,
transferring them from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE asset
accounts, while also ensuring the accurate reflection of corresponding
depreciation charges. Additionally, strengthen internal controls in
monitoring project completion to ensure that similar issues do not recur
in the future, promoting accurate and timely updates to the accounting
records.

c. Review and analyze the recorded Advances to Contractors and Retention
Money or Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable, and effect the necessary
correcting entries to address misclassified CIP accounts and rectify any
erroneous recordings.

2.13 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. General Services Office, in coordination with the Inventory Committee,
to conduct complete annual physical count of all PPEs; and promptly
prepare the Report thereon and to furnish the Office of the Auditor not
later than January 31st of each year;

b. ICO-Municipal Accountant to prioritize the completion and maintenance
of PPELCs or equivalent documents to facilitate reconciliation of records
with the GSO; and

c. OIC-General Services Office and the ICO-Municipal Accountant to
continually update and reconcile records, thereafter, make necessary
adjustments on any discrepancies noted to enable appropriate
presentation of PPE account balances in the financial statements.
Additionally, the procedures for one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances should be reviewed and implemented in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

3.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the ICO-
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare depreciation schedules that are complete with essential data to
enable appropriate computation of depreciation expenses;

b. Review and evaluate all depreciable PPE accounts and prepare necessary
adjusting journal entries relative thereto; and

c. Ensure that the accumulated depreciation accounts are properly
supported with lapsing schedules to enable straightforward verification
of the associated account balances in the financial statements.

4.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

a. Direct the ICO-Municipal Accountant to make appropriate journal
entries to adjust the book balance of the Cash in Bank Local Currency
Current Account for the reconciling items identified.

b. Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to identify and notify the payees with stale checks in the bank
reconciliation statements that are long outstanding, for cancellation and
replacement - if warranted, with corresponding adjustments in the books
for those identified with complete supporting documents for a more
accurate recording.

5.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the ICO-
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal
Treasurer, to:

a.) Exert all necessary efforts to retrieve or reconstruct the pertinent
documents related to the fund transfers, particularly those with
undetermined status, to properly validate their nature and ensure the
accuracy and reliability of the recorded balances.

b.) Ensure that all fund balances identified as completed projects be properly
liquidated or refunded to the source agency, in compliance with COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

5.12 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to provide guidance
to all concerned departments to expedite the implementation of the programs
and projects currently marked as "for implementation," to ensure the timely
and efficient utilization of the transferred and/or downloaded funds.

6.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Acting
Municipal Treasurer to regularly remit the amounts due to the BIR, GSIS,
Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth within the prescribed remittance period, pursuant
to Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 58(A) of the NIRC, Section 6(b) of R.A
No. 8291, R.A. No. 7875 (as amended by R.A. No. 9241) and R.A. No. 7742,
to ensure the accompanying benefits are afforded and avoid imposition of
penalties and surcharges.

6.11 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the ICO-
Municipal Accountant to press on the ongoing efforts to retrieve or
reconstruct the necessary documents for the proper reconciliation of long
outstanding inter-agency payables, thereby ensuring the timely remittance
of dues to the appropriate government financial institutions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct officials and employees concerned to liquidate or refund immediately the cash advances granted to them pursuant to the above-mentioned regulations. BAC Chairperson and Members to adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 on all procurement activities of the municipality and ensure that the supporting documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement vouchers. 

Concerned officials and employees to stop the practice of paying through cash advances in the procurement of goods and other services for the implementation of municipal programs, projects, and activities. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Chairperson direct the Members of Local Council for Protection of Children (LCPC) prepare and submit the Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC) and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board, in coordination with GAD Focal Person to adequately program and allocate from the Special Education Fund (SEF) and Gender and Development (GAD) Fund the PPAs intended for the said purpose.

We further recommended that the foregoing officials adhere to Section 7, Rule VII of the IRR of RA No. 10410 and Items 3.4 and 4.1.7 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s.2017, dated January 19, 2017 to ensure optimum attainment of the purpose, goals and objectives of the ECCD program.

We further recommended that the ICO - Municipal Treasurer and ICO - Municipal Accountant submit to the LSB certified statement containing the following: 1) actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; (2) actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; (3) the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and (4) the annual estimates for the ensuing year not later than July 15 of the current year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Chairperson of the LDRRM Council direct the LDRRM Officer III to comply with the minimum standards for equipage which should be purchased/owned and be maintained by the LDRRM Office of the municipality. We further recommended that the LDRRM Council through LDRRM Officer III in coordination with MPDO and MEO design and program a permanent and disaster resilient evacuation center (multipurpose center) to be included in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and LDRRM Plan consistent with the minimum standards set by the NDRRM Council.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Sangguniang Bayan conduct periodic review of the operations of LEEs and evaluate the efficiency of the revenue collections. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairperson and Members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to faithfully adhere to the above-cited laws, rules and regulations and stop the practice of splitting purchase orders or contracts to avoid offenses and penalties stated in the penal clause of the said law. Proper procurement planning is the solution.
We also recommended that the Chairperson and Members of the BAC provide justification for not adhering to procedures or guidelines set by the 2009 as amended by 2016 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 specifically Rule XVI, Section 54.1.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2018-009 dated February 26, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Engineer to submit within the reglementary period copies of pertinent contracts and documents as enumerated above together with the other specific requirements as mentioned in the annexes of the said circular for technical review/inspection thereof.
We also recommended that the municipality adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 for a timely evaluation of the programs, projects and activities to avoid suspensions in audit.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2018-005 dated February 20, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads to adopt the existing manual in the planning, budgeting, implementing and reporting of the LEEs as prescribed by the above-cited manual.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Sangguniang Bayan conduct periodic review of the operations of LEEs and evaluate the profitability and self-sufficiency (not dependent to subsidy, else, they would be treated public facility/utility) of the individual LEEs. Amendments of Revenue Code involving collections of fees by LEEs for a more viable and profitable operations maybe considered, notwithstanding the services provided therein.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2018-011 dated February 26, 2018.

We recommend that issuances of medicines and medical supplies be done through issuance of RIS and their receipts and utilization be record in a standard inventory ledger card showing the quantities received, issued and balance. Pending the finalization and issuance of a new manual for local government accounting and auditing, the manner of recording of supplies inventory should be aligned to that of Section 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs for fair presentation of concerned accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the assigned department head/s or personnel to fast track the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI), attached as “Annex IV” of the CY 2015 Annual Audit Report (attached herewith for ready reference), to the auditor concerned to facilitate monitoring and validations of the actions taken by the LGU on the related audit observations and recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report to the Regional Director, COA Region VI, for monitoring purpose. Compliance on the submission of the same report to the above-mentioned oversight bodies should be made.    

Moreover, compliance is necessary to avoid further penalties as provided on this act.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the ICO - Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and  LDRRM Officer III through LDRRMC to stop disbursing and appropriating payment of registration fees and insurance policy coverage that were not considered as Acts of God/Nature in accordance with item 3.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRM Officer III to require the curriculum vitae of each of the lecturers/resource persons/coordinators/facilitators in order to substantiate the expertise, difficulty and complexity of the subject matter, the professional qualifications and the position levels of participants to suitably assess the competencies and to appropriately determine the amount of honorarium to be paid. 

We further recommended that the LDRRM Officer III prepare a course syllabus/program of lectures on every trainings and workshops to be conducted in conformity with Item 5.7.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Else, a contract of services should be provided as basis of measuring accomplishments and as reference of hourly or daily rates of their honoraria.

We likewise recommended that the ICO - Municipal Accountant and  LDRRM Officer III apply the formula as a guide in the computation of the honorarium paid to the lecturers/resource persons/coordinators/facilitators in accordance with the guidelines set forth by Section 4.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007.

We recommend that the ICO - Municipal Accountant and Local School Board discontinue the practice of appropriating and disbursing honorarium to LSB teachers not classified as extension classes and subsidy of participants during the CDSA fellowship night and similar expenses against the Special Education Fund. 

We also recommend that the Chairperson, Municipal Local School Board prioritize in the budget preparation the expenses chargeable to Special Education Fund in accordance with the above-cited implementing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and ICO-Municipal Accountant grant cash advances only to the Designated Disbursing Officer or Special Disbursing Officer of the Municipality for time-bound activities pursuant to Item 4.1.3 (v) COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommend that the municipality refrain from charging expenditures for maintenance and other operating expenses on the 20% MDF. Furthermore, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, as well as the Budget Officer, should allocate under the said fund those development projects and programs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures as embodied in the foregoing guidelines.

Also, we recommend that expenses for trainings, printings and the like should be appropriated and charged against the MOOE of appropriate funds other than that of the MDF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to strengthen its commitments in adopting a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program. A regular meeting shall be made to keep abreast with the activities listed on their timeline so that necessary actions be made in areas where there is a need for improvement and to ensure that the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan will be implemented accordingly. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board formulate and adopt revenue-generating measures to ensure the viability of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan such as, but not limited to:

   Strengthening the collection of fines and penalties to the households and institutions who violates the said act especially that there is a suspension order on the collection of garbage. 

   Recycling of junk materials and scraps that could be a source of livelihood.

   Promoting organic fertilizers and sale from the vermi-compost to the farmers and other institutions.

Pursue the establishment of an Eco-SWM Center to resume the collection of the garbage from households and business establishments and eventually collecting garbage fees
